card.intuition: A design pattern where an object's dependencies are provided ('injected') from the outside, rather than the object creating them itself.
card.analogy: Building a Lego set: instead of the Lego house having to build its own front door and windows, you (the builder) just snap existing doors and windows (dependencies) onto the house.
when_to_use: Writing unit tests (inject a 'MockDatabase' instead of a real one)
Keeping your code decoupled and easy to refactor
Using frameworks like Spring (Java), NestJS (TS), or Angular
gotchas: Don't confuse DI with a 'Service Locator' — DI is about receiving dependencies, Service Locator is about asking for them
In simple scripts, over-using DI can add unnecessary complexity 'boilerplate' that makes the code harder to read

exercise.prompt: Convert this tightly coupled code into Dependency Injection style.
hints: Pass the 'db' instance into the constructor instead of creating it inside
exercise.test_code:
class MockDB: pass
us = UserService(MockDB())
assert isinstance(us.db, MockDB)
print("PASS")
